title: Sentinel Shield tool-provenance audit report
description: Machine report emitted by scripts/audits/tool-provenance-audit.sh. Records how each scanner tool is acquired (local binary, Docker image, or verified download) with resolved version / digest / platform, and verifies a checksum when one is declared. A non-empty 'violations' array (e.g. a checksum mismatch or a download declared without a checksum) means the gate failed closed.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the audit run.

platform.description: Host platform the audit ran on, "<os>/<arch>".

status.description: pass = zero violations; fail = one or more.

checked.description: Number of tool acquisition specs inspected.

violations.description: One object per detected provenance failure. Empty when status=pass.

check.description: checksum-mismatch = resolved binary's SHA-256 != declared expected; missing-checksum = a download URL was declared with no expected SHA-256 (unverifiable acquisition).

records.description: One provenance record per inspected tool (see isolated_tool_provenance_record).

source.description: How the tool was resolved. unresolved = neither a binary nor an image was found.

binary.description: Present when a local binary (or download) was resolved.

checksum_verified.description: true=verified match, false=mismatch, null=no expected checksum declared.

image.description: Present when a Docker image was resolved.

vulnerability_db.description: Vulnerability database provenance for DB-backed scanners.

timestamp.description: Vulnerability DB build/update timestamp, if known.
